content.thinking:
Let me analyze the three grids:

Target grid:
- Columns 1-2: empty (□)
- Columns 3-5: filled (X)
- This pattern repeats for all 5 rows

Distractor grid 1:
- Columns 1-3: empty (□)
- Columns 4-5: filled (X)
- This pattern repeats for all 5 rows

Distractor grid 2:
- Column 1: filled (X)
- Columns 2-3: empty (□)
- Columns 4-5: filled (X)
- This pattern repeats for all 5 rows

Key differences:
- Target has 3 columns of X on the right
- Distractor 1 has 2 columns of X on the right
- Distractor 2 has 1 column of X on the left and 2 columns of X on the right

The target can be distinguished by:
- Having exactly 3 consecutive columns of filled cells on the right side
- Having exactly 2 consecutive columns of empty cells on the left side
- The X's start from column 3

Expression options:
- "Three columns of filled cells on the right side"
- "The rightmost three columns are filled"
- "Two empty columns on the left, three filled columns on the right"

content.text: Expression: Three consecutive columns of filled cells on the right side with two empty columns on the left
